statement: INSERT INTO evaluator (user_id, name, judge_prompt, prompt_parser, response_parser, class_config, judge_models, client_side) VALUES (%(user_id)s, %(name)s, %(judge_prompt)s, %(prompt_parser)s, %(response_parser)s, %(class_config)s, %(judge_models)s, %(client_side)s) RETURNING evaluator.id

parameters.name: test_eval
parameters.judge_prompt:
{"messages":[{"content":"[System]
Please act as an impartial judge and evaluate the quality of the response provided by an assistant to the user question displayed below.
Your job is to evaluate how good the assistant's answer is.
Your evaluation should consider correctness and helpfulness. Identify any mistakes.

Be as objective as possible.","role":"system"},{"content":"
[start of user question]
{user_message}
[end of user quesstion]

[start of assistant answer]
{assistant_message}
[end of assistant answer]","role":"user"}],"frequency_penalty":null,"logit_bias":null,"logprobs":null,"top_logprobs":null,"max_completion_tokens":null,"n":null,"presence_penalty":null,"response_format":null,"seed":null,"stop":null,"temperature":null,"top_p":null,"tools":null,"tool_choice":null,"parallel_tool_calls":null,"extra_headers":null,"extra_query":null,"extra_body":null}

INSERT INTO stored_prompt_response (datum_id, prompt_variation_id, endpoint_str, response, num_tokens) SELECT p0::INTEGER, p1::INTEGER, p2::VARCHAR, p3::VARCHAR, p4::INTEGER FROM (VALUES (%(datum_id__0)s, %(prompt_variation_id__0)s, %(endpoint_str__0)s, %(response__0)s, %(num_tokens__0)s, 0), (%(datum_id__1)s, %(prompt_variation_id__1)s, %(endpoint_str__1)s, %(response__1)s, %(num_tokens__1)s, 1)) AS imp_sen(p0, p1, p2, p3, p4, sen_counter) ORDER BY sen_counter RETURNING stored_prompt_response.id, stored_prompt_response.id AS id__1

parameters.response: {"model": "llama-3-8b-chat@aws-bedrock", "created": 1727705212, "id": "chatcmpl-252188cd-7a4a-4df1-b708-8eb3cafd6cb7", "object": "chat.completion", "usage": {"completion_tokens": 117, "prompt_tokens": 30, "total_tokens": 147, "completion_tokens_details": null, "cost": 7.92e-05}, "choices": [{"finish_reason": "stop", "index": 0, "message": {"content": "\n\nThe half-life of carbon-14 (14C) is approximately 5,730 years. This is the time it takes for half of the original amount of 14C to decay into 14N. Radiocarbon dating is a technique used to determine the age of organic materials by measuring the amount of 14C remaining in the sample. Since the Earth's atmosphere contains a constant amount of 14C, the amount of 14C in a sample can be compared to the amount in the atmosphere at the time of the sample's creation to determine its age.", "role": "assistant", "tool_calls": null, "function_call": null}}]}
